behavior_summary: This script implements the 'U8P_SHIELD::look' (examine) handler for the SHIELD object. When the player examines the item, the script emits a single short player-facing bark: "shield ".
After emitting the bark the process suspends/ends immediately; there is no branching, menu, or external interaction in this script.
The object metadata includes an item property armourClass = 4; this is not read or modified by the script but affects game mechanics elsewhere (armor calculation, defense).
notable_control_flow: U8P_SHIELD::look is a process (process_type 0x0208) that runs once when the object is examined, issues a Bark node, then suspends/terminates the process.
No conditionals, menus, or external calls are present in the decompiled nodes; two Unknown nodes appear in the raw but do not add observable runtime branching.
open_questions: Was U8P_SHIELD::look also ever used as a fast-area entry/exit trigger in engine scripts? (No fast-area nodes present; this is only an inference from process type.)
Are there any other engine-level hooks that display a longer description elsewhere, or is this single-bark the only in-script description?
Does the armourClass = 4 metadata ever get referenced by other systems at moments when this object is interacted with (inferred but not visible in this script)?

main_function.name: U8P_SHIELD::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short inspect/U8P_SHIELD::look description for the SHIELD object by emitting a single bark.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player uses the 'U8P_SHIELD::look' (examine) action on the SHIELD object (inferred from function classification and process_type 0x0208).
May also be invoked by engine-level U8P_SHIELD::look/inspection triggers; no explicit fast-area nodes are present in the script.
